claim: Aegis is a zk-SNARK based rollup that batches thousands of transactions into a single succinct proof verified on the underlying settlement chain.

supporting_evidence.quote: Aegis is a zk-SNARK based rollup that batches thousands of transactions into a single succinct proof verified on the underlying settlement chain.

claim: Aegis uses the Groth-Delta proving system, a pairing-based SNARK construction descended from the Groth16 family but extended with a universal, updatable reference string.

supporting_evidence.quote: Aegis uses the Groth-Delta proving system, a pairing-based SNARK construction descended from the Groth16 family but extended with a universal, updatable reference string.

claim: Signature verification dominates the constraint count, since checking an elliptic-curve signature inside a SNARK circuit is substantially more expensive than checking it natively.

supporting_evidence.quote: Signature verification dominates the constraint count, since checking an elliptic-curve signature inside a SNARK circuit is substantially more expensive than checking it natively.

claim: A full batch circuit for two thousand transactions currently contains just under nine million constraints, most of which come from signature verification.

supporting_evidence.quote: A full batch circuit for two thousand transactions currently contains just under nine million constraints, most of which come from signature verification.

claim: Rather than proving an entire day's worth of transactions in one enormous circuit, Aegis composes many small batch proofs recursively into a single final proof.

supporting_evidence.quote: Rather than proving an entire day's worth of transactions in one enormous circuit, Aegis composes many small batch proofs recursively into a single final proof.

claim: Each recursive step verifies two prior proofs inside a new circuit and emits one proof attesting that both inputs were valid, halving the number of outstanding proofs at each level.

supporting_evidence.quote: Each recursive step verifies two prior proofs inside a new circuit and emits one proof attesting that both inputs were valid, halving the number of outstanding proofs at each level.

claim: Security holds as long as at least one participant across the entire ceremony genuinely destroyed their randomness, an assumption the literature calls the one-honest-participant model.

supporting_evidence.quote: Security holds as long as at least one participant across the entire ceremony genuinely destroyed their randomness, an assumption the literature calls the one-honest-participant model.

claim: Aegis livestreamed its ceremony contributions and published verifiable transcripts so that any outside party could confirm a specific contribution was correctly incorporated.

supporting_evidence.quote: Aegis livestreamed its ceremony contributions and published verifiable transcripts so that any outside party could confirm a specific contribution was correctly incorporated.

claim: Verification, in contrast, requires only a handful of pairing operations regardless of circuit size, which is precisely the succinctness property that makes on-chain verification practical.

supporting_evidence.quote: Verification, in contrast, requires only a handful of pairing operations regardless of circuit size, which is precisely the succinctness property that makes on-chain verification practical.

claim: This asymmetry is deliberate: the protocol pushes all the expensive work onto a small number of provers who are compensated for it, while every verifying node pays only a small, fixed cost.

supporting_evidence.quote: This asymmetry is deliberate: the protocol pushes all the expensive work onto a small number of provers who are compensated for it, while every verifying node pays only a small, fixed cost.

claim: STARK-based rollups avoid any trusted setup entirely, relying only on collision-resistant hashing rather than pairing-based cryptography.

supporting_evidence.quote: STARK-based rollups avoid any trusted setup entirely, relying only on collision-resistant hashing rather than pairing-based cryptography.

claim: This comes at the cost of larger proofs: a typical STARK proof is several times larger than the equivalent Groth-Delta proof for a comparable computation.

supporting_evidence.quote: This comes at the cost of larger proofs: a typical STARK proof is several times larger than the equivalent Groth-Delta proof for a comparable computation.

claim: In production, Aegis separates the prover network from the sequencer that orders incoming transactions, allowing each component to scale independently.

supporting_evidence.quote: In production, Aegis separates the prover network from the sequencer that orders incoming transactions, allowing each component to scale independently.

claim: Multiple provers can work on the same batch redundantly, with only the first valid proof submitted actually accepted by the settlement chain's verifying contract.

supporting_evidence.quote: Multiple provers can work on the same batch redundantly, with only the first valid proof submitted actually accepted by the settlement chain's verifying contract.

claim: Aegis 1.0 launched in April 2021 with a maximum batch size of 500 transactions and proving times averaging eighteen minutes per batch.

supporting_evidence.quote: Aegis 1.0 launched in April 2021 with a maximum batch size of 500 transactions and proving times averaging eighteen minutes per batch.

claim: Aegis 2.0, shipped in June 2022, upgraded to the Groth-Delta proving system from an earlier, non-updatable predecessor construction.

supporting_evidence.quote: Aegis 2.0, shipped in June 2022, upgraded to the Groth-Delta proving system from an earlier, non-updatable predecessor construction.

claim: Aegis 2.4, released in March 2023, raised the maximum batch size to 4,000 transactions after the signature-embedding optimization described in the circuit design section.

supporting_evidence.quote: Aegis 2.4, released in March 2023, raised the maximum batch size to 4,000 transactions after the signature-embedding optimization described in the circuit design section.

claim: The Aegis Groth-Delta ceremony ran across fourteen months, from January 2022 through February 2023, before the reference string was declared final.

supporting_evidence.quote: The Aegis Groth-Delta ceremony ran across fourteen months, from January 2022 through February 2023, before the reference string was declared final.

claim: A total of 187 independent parties contributed randomness during that window, including universities, cryptography firms, and individual researchers.

supporting_evidence.quote: A total of 187 independent parties contributed randomness during that window, including universities, cryptography firms, and individual researchers.

claim: Contribution proofs and the full transcript remain published and independently verifiable, and no participant has ever come forward claiming a break in the resulting reference string.

supporting_evidence.quote: Contribution proofs and the full transcript remain published and independently verifiable, and no participant has ever come forward claiming a break in the resulting reference string.

claim: Groth-Delta's design has been evaluated against several earlier proving systems using a shared trusted-setup benchmark tracked by the wider proof-systems research community.

supporting_evidence.quote: Groth-Delta's design has been evaluated against several earlier proving systems using a shared trusted-setup benchmark tracked by the wider proof-systems research community.

claim: One frequently cited comparison point is participant count: a well-known formally verified implementation study of a Groth-Delta-style ceremony reported 199 participants contributing to its own instantiation of the protocol.

supporting_evidence.quote: One frequently cited comparison point is participant count: a well-known formally verified implementation study of a Groth-Delta-style ceremony reported 199 participants contributing to its own instantiation of the protocol.

claim: Researchers comparing ceremony sizes generally treat higher participant counts as evidence of a stronger one-honest-participant assumption, though the relationship is not strictly linear.

supporting_evidence.quote: Researchers comparing ceremony sizes generally treat higher participant counts as evidence of a stronger one-honest-participant assumption, though the relationship is not strictly linear.

claim: Sable is a separate zk-rollup network that adopted an early, pre-updatable variant of the proving techniques Aegis later built on.

supporting_evidence.quote: Sable is a separate zk-rollup network that adopted an early, pre-updatable variant of the proving techniques Aegis later built on.

claim: Sable's mainnet launched in 2022, several months after its own independent, single-ceremony trusted setup was finalized.

supporting_evidence.quote: Sable's mainnet launched in 2022, several months after its own independent, single-ceremony trusted setup was finalized.

claim: Unlike Aegis, Sable never migrated to an updatable reference string, and its ceremony participant list has remained fixed since mainnet launch.

supporting_evidence.quote: Unlike Aegis, Sable never migrated to an updatable reference string, and its ceremony participant list has remained fixed since mainnet launch.

claim: Comparing proof size and verification time across proving systems clarifies the tradeoffs Aegis's designers weighed before choosing Groth-Delta.

supporting_evidence.quote: Comparing proof size and verification time across proving systems clarifies the tradeoffs Aegis's designers weighed before choosing Groth-Delta.

claim: Verification time matters most for on-chain costs, since every verification consumes gas on the settlement chain regardless of how the proof itself was generated.

supporting_evidence.quote: Verification time matters most for on-chain costs, since every verification consumes gas on the settlement chain regardless of how the proof itself was generated.

claim: Proving time scales with circuit size, which in turn scales with batch size, as the following measurements from the 2.4 release illustrate.

supporting_evidence.quote: Proving time scales with circuit size, which in turn scales with batch size, as the following measurements from the 2.4 release illustrate.

claim: The final row demonstrates that proving parallelizes well across multiple GPUs, since the multi-scalar multiplication step splits cleanly across independent shards of the witness.

supporting_evidence.quote: The final row demonstrates that proving parallelizes well across multiple GPUs, since the multi-scalar multiplication step splits cleanly across independent shards of the witness.

claim: Aegis's circuit auditors follow a fixed checklist before approving any change to the batch circuit for production deployment.

supporting_evidence.quote: Aegis's circuit auditors follow a fixed checklist before approving any change to the batch circuit for production deployment.

claim: No circuit change reaches production without every item on this checklist signed off by at least two separate auditors.

supporting_evidence.quote: No circuit change reaches production without every item on this checklist signed off by at least two separate auditors.

claim: Prospective ceremony contributors had to satisfy a short list of requirements before their contribution was accepted into the sequence.

supporting_evidence.quote: Prospective ceremony contributors had to satisfy a short list of requirements before their contribution was accepted into the sequence.

claim: A handful of prospective contributors were rejected for failing to meet the disconnected-machine requirement, and were invited to redo their contribution under the correct conditions.

supporting_evidence.quote: A handful of prospective contributors were rejected for failing to meet the disconnected-machine requirement, and were invited to redo their contribution under the correct conditions.

claim: Chen and Liu's 简洁非交互式知识论证的构造 (2013) lays out an early construction whose structure Groth-Delta's designers cite as a direct conceptual ancestor.

supporting_evidence.quote: Chen and Liu's 简洁非交互式知识论证的构造 (2013) lays out an early construction whose structure Groth-Delta's designers cite as a direct conceptual ancestor.

claim: Sokolova's Свойства парных билинейных отображений в криптографии (2015) formalizes several of the bilinear pairing properties Groth-Delta's soundness proof relies on.

supporting_evidence.quote: Sokolova's Свойства парных билинейных отображений в криптографии (2015) formalizes several of the bilinear pairing properties Groth-Delta's soundness proof relies on.

claim: The bilinear pairing e: G1 x G2 -> GT is sometimes written with a Cyrillic-influenced symbol in older Russian-language papers, б, to denote the same target group GT.

supporting_evidence.quote: The bilinear pairing e: G1 x G2 -> GT is sometimes written with a Cyrillic-influenced symbol in older Russian-language papers, б, to denote the same target group GT.

claim: Greek-language treatments typically retain the Latin e but use φ (phi) rather than the Latin f for the associated isomorphism between G1 and G2.

supporting_evidence.quote: Greek-language treatments typically retain the Latin e but use φ (phi) rather than the Latin f for the associated isomorphism between G1 and G2.

claim: Let e: G1 x G2 -> GT be the bilinear pairing, and let A, B, C be the three group elements that make up the proof itself.

supporting_evidence.quote: Let e: G1 x G2 -> GT be the bilinear pairing, and let A, B, C be the three group elements that make up the proof itself.

claim: Evaluating this equation requires only three pairing computations regardless of circuit size, which is the source of Groth-Delta's constant-time verification.

supporting_evidence.quote: Evaluating this equation requires only three pairing computations regardless of circuit size, which is the source of Groth-Delta's constant-time verification.

claim: The transform replaces a verifier's random challenge with a hash of the protocol transcript so far, computed by the prover itself.

supporting_evidence.quote: The transform replaces a verifier's random challenge with a hash of the protocol transcript so far, computed by the prover itself.

claim: Soundness of this substitution holds in the random oracle model, where the hash function is treated as an idealized random function for the purposes of the security proof.

supporting_evidence.quote: Soundness of this substitution holds in the random oracle model, where the hash function is treated as an idealized random function for the purposes of the security proof.

claim: The first major improvement came from replacing a naive signature-verification gadget with one specifically optimized for the target elliptic curve, cutting per-transaction constraint count by roughly two-thirds.

supporting_evidence.quote: The first major improvement came from replacing a naive signature-verification gadget with one specifically optimized for the target elliptic curve, cutting per-transaction constraint count by roughly two-thirds.

claim: The choice of proving system itself changed twice before settling on Groth-Delta: the original prototype used a simpler, non-updatable pairing-based scheme, since building an updatable reference string was considered out of scope for a research prototype.

supporting_evidence.quote: The choice of proving system itself changed twice before settling on Groth-Delta: the original prototype used a simpler, non-updatable pairing-based scheme, since building an updatable reference string was considered out of scope for a research prototype.

claim: Looking back, the team identifies the signature-embedding optimization and the eventual move to an updatable reference string as the two decisions that mattered most for reaching production scale.

supporting_evidence.quote: Looking back, the team identifies the signature-embedding optimization and the eventual move to an updatable reference string as the two decisions that mattered most for reaching production scale.

claim: In March 2023, shortly after the 2.4 release raised the maximum batch size to 4,000 transactions, prover operators began reporting proving times nearly double the numbers published in the release benchmarks.

supporting_evidence.quote: In March 2023, shortly after the 2.4 release raised the maximum batch size to 4,000 transactions, prover operators began reporting proving times nearly double the numbers published in the release benchmarks.

claim: Multi-signature transactions triggered a code path that fell back to a less efficient, unoptimized signature gadget, one the optimization work had not been extended to cover.

supporting_evidence.quote: Multi-signature transactions triggered a code path that fell back to a less efficient, unoptimized signature gadget, one the optimization work had not been extended to cover.

claim: No funds were ever at risk during the incident, since a slow proof delays settlement rather than compromising the correctness of any state transition the circuit proves.

supporting_evidence.quote: No funds were ever at risk during the incident, since a slow proof delays settlement rather than compromising the correctness of any state transition the circuit proves.

claim: This recap summarizes the proof-system tradeoff for readers who only skimmed the overview section of the specification.

supporting_evidence.quote: This recap summarizes the proof-system tradeoff for readers who only skimmed the overview section of the specification.

claim: The exact percentage varies slightly depending on the specific circuit being measured, but the broad tradeoff between ceremony flexibility and proof size holds consistently across circuits.

supporting_evidence.quote: The exact percentage varies slightly depending on the specific circuit being measured, but the broad tradeoff between ceremony flexibility and proof size holds consistently across circuits.

claim: This recap consolidates the version-history highlights most frequently requested by teams evaluating Aegis for a new integration.

supporting_evidence.quote: This recap consolidates the version-history highlights most frequently requested by teams evaluating Aegis for a new integration.

claim: Every release since 2.0 has used Groth-Delta exclusively, with no further changes to the underlying proving system through the most recent release.

supporting_evidence.quote: Every release since 2.0 has used Groth-Delta exclusively, with no further changes to the underlying proving system through the most recent release.

claim: A Groth16 proof is 192 bytes, verifies in 2 ms, and requires a per-circuit trusted setup.

supporting_evidence.quote: | Groth16 | 192 bytes | 2 ms | Per-circuit |

claim: A STARK proof is 45 KB, verifies in 12 ms, and requires no trusted setup.

supporting_evidence.quote: | STARK | 45 KB | 12 ms | None |

claim: A 4000-transaction batch of 17.6 million constraints proves in 26 minutes on one A100 GPU.
A 4000-transaction batch proves in 7 minutes on four A100 GPUs.
Groth-Delta verification checks the pairing equation e(A, B) = e(alpha, beta) * e(sum_i(a_i * L_i), gamma) * e(C, delta).

supporting_evidence.quote: e(A, B) = e(alpha, beta) * e(sum_i(a_i * L_i), gamma) * e(C, delta)

claim: The Fiat-Shamir transcript must include every public value the challenge could depend on; omissions have broken soundness in other protocols.

supporting_evidence.quote: A known subtlety is that the transcript must include every public value the challenge could plausibly depend on; omitting one has broken soundness in other, unrelated protocols in the past.

claim: Aegis 1.2, released in November 2021, introduced recursive composition and cut end-to-end settlement latency by more than half.

supporting_evidence.quote: Aegis 1.2, released in November 2021, introduced the recursive composition scheme described earlier, cutting end-to-end settlement latency by more than half.

claim: The ceremony's total wall-clock compute across all contributions exceeded 40,000 core-hours.

supporting_evidence.quote: The ceremony's total wall-clock compute time across all contributions exceeded 40,000 core-hours, most of it spent on the multi-scalar multiplication step each contribution required.

claim: Aegis's engineers studied Sable's early production incidents, particularly a prover downtime incident in Sable's first month, while designing sequencer redundancy.

supporting_evidence.quote: Aegis's engineering team studied Sable's early production incidents closely while designing their own sequencer redundancy scheme, particularly a widely discussed prover downtime incident during Sable's first month.

claim: The 199-participant count and Aegis's own ceremony statistics describe independent instantiations of the same underlying construction.

supporting_evidence.quote: That participant count is often quoted alongside Aegis's own ceremony statistics, even though the two ceremonies are independent instantiations of the same underlying construction.

claim: Any circuit constraint count change exceeding five percent requires an independent audit.

supporting_evidence.quote: 4. Obtain an independent audit of any constraint count change exceeding five percent.

claim: Ceremony contributors had to publish a signed attestation that the contributing machine was disconnected from any network during randomness generation.

supporting_evidence.quote: 3. Publish a signed attestation confirming the contributing machine was disconnected from any network during randomness generation.

claim: Dimitriou's study of interactive proof systems traces the path from interactive proofs to the non-interactive constructions used in production.

supporting_evidence.quote: Dimitriou's study of διαδραστικά συστήματα απόδειξης (interactive proof systems) traces the historical path from interactive proofs to the non-interactive constructions used in production today.

claim: Chinese-language papers gloss the pairing operation as 配对 alongside the Latin symbol e rather than introducing separate notation.

supporting_evidence.quote: Chinese-language papers from the same period frequently use 配对 (pairing) as a gloss alongside the Latin symbol e, rather than introducing a separate notation entirely.

claim: The retrospective quantified the regression's total impact at roughly 60 hours of delayed settlement across affected batches.

supporting_evidence.quote: A retrospective published alongside the 2.4.1 patch quantified the regression's total impact at roughly 60 hours of delayed settlement across affected batches over the incident window.
